author: Alex Zislin
executive_summary: Alex Zislin, a self-described active small investor, opposes the proposed changes to the Trade-Through Rule and Locked and Crossed Markets Provisions of Regulation NMS (File No. S7-2026-20), citing the importance of best price execution to his personal portfolio performance.

positions.best_execution: Strongly supportive of maintaining best execution protections; opposes changes that may weaken them

economic_arguments: Best price execution is critical to maximizing retail investor portfolio returns
Changes to the Trade-Through Rule may negatively impact the prices retail investors receive on their orders
retail_investor_arguments: As an active small investor, the author has a direct personal stake in best execution outcomes
Proposed changes to Regulation NMS Trade-Through Rule protections are seen as harmful to retail investors who rely on order protection rules to receive the best available prices
recommended_actions: Reject or withdraw the proposed changes to the Trade-Through Rule and Locked and Crossed Markets Provisions of Regulation NMS
key_quotes: I wish to register opposition to the proposed changes to S7-2026-20 == The Trade-Through Rule and Locked and Crossed Markets Provisions of Regulation NMS.
As an active small investor, best price execution of orders is extremely important to maximizing my portfolio.
